behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real model judgment quality and linguistic coverage, not just routing or schema wiring. MOCK-MISS: this is not mainly a module handoff bug. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess requirement beyond normal API call semantics. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness is about whether the model actually finds terms in varied legal text; stubs would hide misses. MULTI-STEP: no, this is one reviewer step. Rejected higher level Workflow because behavior text is single-step extractor quality, not a chained agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on a curated set of real contracts with gold annotations for parties/dates/obligations/penalties, and fail if recall/precision drops below threshold or key term categories are systematically missed.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: Correctness hinges on semantic interpretation of indirect/euphemistic wording by a real model. MOCK-MISS: a stubbed classifier would pass by construction and miss true detection failures. REAL-INFRA: no special OS/subprocess boundary is core here. LLM-DEP: explicitly yes, quality of model judgment under nuanced language. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agent chain required for this behavior alone. Rejected Workflow because behavior is one reviewer’s assessment task.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real risk-assessor model against a labeled dataset containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses under the configured policy, asserting minimum detection quality and low false negatives on indirect phrasing.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: This is model-dependent legal-compliance judgment quality for jurisdiction-specific requirements. MOCK-MISS: deterministic mocks cannot expose misclassification of compliance status. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/subprocess boundary in the behavior itself. LLM-DEP: yes, outcome depends on real AI interpretation of legal text and rules. MULTI-STEP: described as a single checker output, not a chained agent process. Rejected Workflow because behavior text scopes to one checker decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance-checker model on jurisdiction-tagged contracts with expert-labeled compliance outcomes and requirement-level expectations; fail on incorrect overall verdicts or missed mandatory requirements.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (e.g., extractor says "standard" but risk assessor says "high risk"), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is module handoff/schema alignment between reviewer outputs and merge logic. MOCK-MISS: pure unit tests of merge internals can miss interface mismatches (IDs, clause normalization, attribution fields) across modules. REAL-INFRA check: no real OS/network/subprocess is required. LLM-DEP check: behavior does not require real model quality; conflicting inputs can be deterministic fixtures. MULTI-STEP check: not multiple real agentic steps, only merge handling. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is about preserving conflicting assessments with attribution in merge semantics, not infrastructure behavior or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire reviewer output adapters plus merge module, feed deterministic conflicting clause outputs from two reviewers, and assert unified report retains both assessments with correct reviewer attribution and no overwrite.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging into a unified report — completes correctly for a real contract without losing any individual reviewer's findings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans multiple reviewer steps plus merge across the full pipeline, and reviewers are AI components. MULTI-STEP: yes, several agentic steps in one end-to-end chain. LLM-DEP: yes, each reviewer output is model-produced. MOCK-MISS: mocking reviewers can hide coordination/loss issues in real asynchronous agent outputs. REAL-INFRA may exist, but Workflow is higher and better matches the failure boundary. Rejected Agentic as too low because behavior text requires end-to-end orchestration across three reviewers and merge, not single-step model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real end-to-end pipeline on real contracts, invoking all three live reviewers in parallel, then verify the final merged report includes traceable findings from each reviewer with no dropped items during collection or merge.
